data.title: OpenAI brings in OpenClaw founder to spearhead development of personal AI agents, turning it into a foundation.
data.description: The founder announced plans to transform OpenClaw into a foundation, highlighting OpenAI as the most efficient method for making open agents accessible to all.

data.title: Survey by ResumeBuilder.com finds 60% of workers are hesitant to leave jobs in 2026 due to job market fears.
data.description: Concerns about the job market are leading to a notable increase in risk-averse behavior in Guaynabo, Puerto Rico. Recently, ResumeBuilder.com, a leading platform for professional resume templates and career guidance, released a survey report indicating a clear rise in "job hugging." This behavior entails individuals clinging to their current employment due to uncertainties in the job market. The report, released on Feb. 16, 2026, highlights a significant shift in attitudes, with individuals prioritizing job security over potential career advancement opportunities. This trend reflects a growing sense of apprehension surrounding the stability of the job market in the region.

data.title: UK PM wants more powers to regulate AI chatbots due to child safety worries, including setting age limits and feature restrictions.
data.description: AI chatbots will now be subject to UK online safety laws, which will allow for swift implementation of age restrictions and limitations on features to safeguard children.

data.title: Michael Saylor plans to convert Strategy Inc.'s debt into equity over 3-6 years as Bitcoin (BTC) drops to $8,000, but will still cover $6 billion debt with $49 billion in BTC reserves.
data.description: Michael Saylor, the founder of Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ: MSTR), revealed plans to convert the company's convertible debt into equity over the next three to six years. The company, known for hoarding Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), stated that it will not issue new convertible notes. Even if Bitcoin's price were to decrease to $8,000, Strategy assured stakeholders that it holds enough assets to cover its existing debt. With $49 billion in BTC reserves and $6 billion in net debt, calculated as total outside debt minus cash reserves, the firm emphasized its financial stability. In a scenario where Bitcoin's price drops by 88% to $8,000, Strategy's reserves would reduce to around $6 billion, aligning with the net debt figure. The company emphasized its intention to steer clear of additional senior debt issuance through convertible means. This information was reported without specifying the source.

data.title: CEO Stani Kulechov of Aave Labs suggests onchain lending can drive DeFi with a $50T surge in "abundance assets" like solar, robotics, and energy storage.
data.description: The CEO of Aave Labs, Stani Kulechov, expressed that onchain lending has the potential to expedite the progress of industries such as solar power, energy storage, and robotics by providing financial support to "future-proof" assets.

data.title: Mitsubishi Electric renaming Thai subsidiary Siam Compressor Industry to Mitsubishi Electric Siam Compressor Industry Co., Ltd.
data.description: In Tokyo today, Mitsubishi Electric Corporation (TOKYO: 6503) revealed that its Thai subsidiary, Siam Compressor Industry Co., Ltd., specializing in compressors for air conditioning and refrigeration equipment, will be rebranded as Mitsubishi Electric Siam Compressor Industry Co., Ltd. starting April 1. This change reflects their strong presence in the market. Siam Compressor Industry not only serves Mitsubishi Electric group entities but also offers a diverse selection of compressors to various air conditioning suppliers.
